Title: UWA Winthrop Scholarship
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: UWA Winthrop Scholarship
INFO: STATUS: Open | APPLICATIONS OPEN: 05/01/2026 | APPLICATIONS CLOSE: 20/06/2026 | VALUE: $80,000.00 | NUMBER OFFERED: 10

[Overview]
The UWA Winthrop Scholarship is one of our University's Prestigious Scholarships. This scholarship program has been developed to provide high school leavers with an opportunity to undertake an undergraduate degree course and then a postgraduate coursework degree at the University. The scholarship is for graduating high-achieving, high school students. They will show significant academic excellence, are community minded and can demonstrate achievements in a co-curricular activity throughout Year 11 and 12. A statement evidencing their leadership ambitions will also be required.

[Eligibility]
Please note, the current application period for this scholarship is for current year 12 High School students planning on commencing their undergraduate degree at UWA in Semester 1, 2027.

To be eligible to be considered for the scholarship, an applicant must—
(a) be a domestic student;
(b) in the year prior to commencing an undergraduate course at the University—
(i) have accepted an Early Offer to the University, or applied through the Tertiary Institutions Service Centre (TISC) whilst in Year 12 for a place at the University, nominating a course at the University as their first preference in the first round of offers;
(ii) achieve an ATAR of 95 or above
(c) be a commencing student;
(d) be enrolled, or eligible to enrol—
(i) full-time and
(ii) in an undergraduate degree course.

[Benefits]
The UWA Winthrop Scholarship is one of our University's Prestigious Scholarships. Students who show significant academic potential, community-minded behaviours and outstanding achievements throughout Year 11 and 12 in co-curricular activities with an ambition to develop their leadership ability, are encouraged to apply.

Recipients of the scholarship will be invited, and expected, to participate actively in a university prescribed leadership development and mentorship program. This includes membership of the University’s mentor scheme, participation in other selected activities and periodic visits to schools to promote university education in general.

Value: up to $80,000*

$10,000 per annum (six semesters undergraduate plus one year honours if applicable) and a further
$10,000 per annum (maximum eight semesters postgraduate)

(Conditions apply including maintaining a minimum WAM)

Title: UWA VICE-CHANCELLOR'S SCHOLARSHIP
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: UWA VICE-CHANCELLOR'S SCHOLARSHIP
INFO: STATUS: Open | APPLICATIONS OPEN: 01/06/2026 | APPLICATIONS CLOSE: 05/08/2026 | VALUE: $0.00 | NUMBER OFFERED: 12

[Overview]
The UWA Vice-Chancellor’s Scholarship supports high-achieving students from around the world with 50%* or 100% off UWA tuition fees, so you can focus on what matters most: your future. These scholarships are highly competitive, with only a limited number offered each year. If you’re an international student from China, Indonesia, Malaysia or Vietnam and choose to study a selected degree at The University of Western Australia, you could be eligible to apply for this prestigious scholarship.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to be considered for the scholarship, candidates must—
(a) Be an international student;
(b) Be commencing an eligible course specified in schedule 2 of these conditions
(c) have not commenced or discontinued an equivalent level course at the University;
(d) achieve a minimum ATAR equivalent of 98
(e) meet the eligibility criteria specified in Schedule 1 (Eligible Countries) and Schedule 2 (Eligible Courses); and
(f) provide a 250-500 word statement indicating how the scholarship would be beneficial and how it forms a part of future career plans.

[Benefits]
Twelve Scholarships are available per annum
China: 2x 100% Tuition Fees and 2 x 50% Tuition Fees
Indonesia: 2 x 100% Tuition Fees and 2 x 50% Tuition Fees
Malaysia: 1 x 100% Tuition Fees and 1 x 50% Tuition Fees
Vietnam: 1 x 100% Tuition Fees and 1 x 50% Tuition Fees

The maximum duration of the scholarship is for the standard full-time duration of the recipient’s accepted course for which the scholarship is offered.

The scholarship is provided as a fee discount towards eligible course fees.

Title: UWA CONNECT SCHOLARSHIP
Content_For_AI:
TITLE: UWA CONNECT SCHOLARSHIP
INFO: STATUS: Open | APPLICATIONS OPEN: 01/06/2026 | APPLICATIONS CLOSE: 05/08/2026 | VALUE: $21,500.00 | NUMBER OFFERED: 200

[Overview]
The UWA Connect Scholarship is provided by The University of Western Australia (`the University') in conjunction with the UWA Broadway Connect Support Program to assist and encourage eligible Equity applicants to commence and pursue a bachelor's degree at the University.

[Eligibility]
The UWA Connect Scholarship is provided to set students up for success at UWA who come from a disadvantaged background and/or identify as being in an equity group.

To be eligible to be considered for the scholarship, a candidate must-
(a) be a commencing domestic student
(b) have applied to enrol into a bachelor's degree
(c) come from a disadvantaged background and/or identify in an equity group

These may include, but are not limited to:
(i) Coming from a low socioeconomic status area
(ii) Living in regional or remote area
(iii) Is an Australian Indigenous person
(iv) Medical condition or disability that impacts study
(v) Being the first in your family to attend university
(vi) Other financial disadvantaged or personal hardship

[Benefits]
Recipients of the UWA Connect Scholarship will receive:
• $5,000 per annum for 3 years for Undergraduate study and an additional year for Honours if applicable.
• $1,500 up-front payment in their first year for assistance with commencing study at UWA.
• Participation in a wrap-around support program so students can take full advantage of their time at UWA.

[Overview]
The University of Western Australia is offering scholarships to high-achieving students across the globe who study with us. INFO: STATUS: Open | APPLICATIONS OPEN: 02/02/2026 | APPLICATIONS CLOSE: 05/08/2026 | VALUE: $59,700.00 | NUMBER OFFERED: 3

[Overview]
The University of Western Australia ('the University') is providing a full fee waiver scholarship to encourage and assist high-achieving Indigenous candidates to pursue a Master of Business Administration or Graduate Certificate in Business at the University. Eligible applicants to the University are automatically considered and do not need to submit a separate application.

[Eligibility]
To be eligible to be considered for the scholarship, a candidate must—
(a) be a domestic student;
(b) be an Australian Indigenous person; and
(c) have applied for a place in a Master of Business Administration (MBA) or Graduate Certificate within the UWA Business School at the University.
An Australian citizen or permanent resident or holder of a Permanent Humanitarian Visa or a New Zealand citizen.
An Australian Indigenous person is a person who:
• is of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander descent;
• identifies as an Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander; and
• is accepted as an Australian Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander in the community in which he or she lives or has lived.

[Benefits]
The University of Western Australia (‘the University’) is providing a full fee waiver scholarship to encourage and assist high-achieving Indigenous candidates to pursue a Master of Business Administration or Graduate Certificate in Business at the University.

Eligible applicants to the University are automatically considered and do not need to submit a separate application.
